summary: Phase 2 of check-alignment: @spec annotation coverage analysis, requirementâ†”scenario cross-referencing, nested JSON Schema traversal for OpenRPC specs, and schemaâ†”struct validation stubs. Adds 4 new modules (annotations, coverage, requirement_coverage, schema_struct) and extends CLI with --coverage flag and coverage report output.

files_changed.description: Language-agnostic @spec annotation parser. Scans source files for `@spec {path}#{id}` across 5 comment syntaxes (// # -- <!-- /*). Recursively collects source files by extension and returns Vec<SpecAnnotation>.

Coverage analysis module. Cross-references @spec annotations with spec requirement IDs, identifies covered/uncovered requirements, stale annotations pointing to non-existent specs, and computes coverage ratio. Daemon integration for unspecced function detection is stubbed.

Requirementâ†”Scenario cross-reference checker. Extracts R{N} IDs from Requirements tables, collects references from Scenario sections and Test Plan Covers columns, emits orphan_requirement violations for unreferenced requirements.
Schemaâ†”Struct validation stub. Placeholder for daemon-dependent JSON Schema properties vs Rust struct field comparison. Includes json_schema_type_to_rust() mapping utility.

Extended with Phase 2 data structures: SpecAnnotation, CoverageEntry, UnspeccedFunction, OrphanRequirementEntry, CoverageReport, SchemaStructMismatchEntry. Added 8 new ViolationKind variants (UncoveredRequirement, UnspeccedFunction, StaleAnnotation, OrphanRequirement, SchemaStructMismatch, NestedSchemaConflictRequired, NestedSchemaConflictFieldName, NestedSchemaConflictSchema). Added optional coverage field to CheckResult.
Registered 4 new submodules (annotations, coverage, requirement_coverage, schema_struct). Added check_with_coverage to public API. Updated module doc to describe three-layer validation.

Added check_with_coverage() entry point that runs Phase 1 checks plus Phase 2: annotation coverage analysis, requirementâ†”scenario cross-ref, schemaâ†”struct validation, and daemon readiness detection. Existing check() function unchanged except adding coverage: None to result.

Added R19: nested schema traversal rule. New check_nested_schema_conflicts() traverses result.schema and params[*].schema paths, checking required array conflicts and property type/enum/format conflicts across duplicate OpenRPC definitions.

Extended run() with coverage and source_dirs parameters. When --coverage is enabled, calls check_with_coverage(). Added Phase 2 output formatting: coverage ratio, uncovered requirements, unspecced functions, stale annotations, orphan requirements, combined summary.
Added --coverage flag and --source-dir repeatable arg to CheckAlignment command. Updated dispatch to pass new parameters to check_alignment::run().
Added @spec annotation instruction to implementation agent prompt template, so generated code includes spec traceability annotations.

R1.notes: Language-agnostic scan across 5 comment syntaxes, regex-based extraction, recursive directory scanning with extension filter

R2.notes: Full cross-reference of annotationsâ†”requirements, stale detection, coverage ratio calculation. Unspecced function detection stubbed pending daemon.

R3.notes: detect_unspecced_functions() is a stub â€” requires daemon symbol index integration. Returns empty Vec when daemon not ready.

R4.notes: Stub with json_schema_type_to_rust() mapping utility. Full implementation requires daemon. check() returns empty results when daemon not ready.

R5.notes: Extracts R{N} from Requirements tables, collects references from Scenarios and Test Plan Covers columns, emits OrphanRequirement violations.

R6.notes: R19 rule: check_nested_schema_conflicts() traverses result.schema and params[*].schema for required conflicts and property type/enum/format conflicts.

R7.notes: Added @spec annotation instruction block to implementation prompt template.

R8.notes: --coverage flag enables Phase 2, --source-dir specifies scan targets. Output includes coverage ratio, uncovered requirements, stale annotations, orphan requirements, unspecced functions.

test_status: Phase 2 modules are primarily integration-level (file scanning, cross-referencing). Unit tests for annotation parser regex and requirement extraction deferred to follow-up. Build verification passes.
notes: R3 (Lens/daemon integration) and R4 (Schemaâ†”Struct) are intentionally stubbed â€” they depend on the daemon symbol index which is a separate infrastructure concern. The stubs return empty results when daemon is not ready, with clear TODO comments for future implementation.
